title: spawn/retire
manager: Dynamic spawn and retire — machines can be created and removed from a population at runtime, with indices never reused so identity stays stable and a retired member's slot never silently becomes someone else. This is the lifecycle management for dynamic systems: fleets grow and shrink, and the no-reuse rule is what keeps routes and references from quietly pointing at the wrong machine after churn.
cs: spawn/retire within a population (§22); indices never reused (stable identity); interacts with routes (W7.16) and supervision (W7.20).
